name: tsl_ownership_inertia
description: [TSL S4 / Class G] Ownership inertia: player places a held object on a surface in turn 2; player examines the area in turn 3 without re-acquiring the object. If CB re-emits the object as player-container on turn 3, TSL acquisition_ungrounded must fire. Probabilistic.

tsl_expected.notes: Class G ownership inertia. Turn 2 player explicitly sets the pendant down — the object should transfer from player to grid/localspace. Turn 3 player examines the altar (no re-acquisition request). If CB sees the pendant described on the altar and re-emits it as player-container, TSL fires acquisition_ungrounded: no parser.action=take, no gate evidence for re-acquisition. TSL alias_candidates may additionally show the pendant resolved to its existing object_id (proving TSL sees this as a known tracked object being erroneously re-claimed rather than a new introduction). Probabilistic: CB may correctly leave the pendant as grid/localspace on turn 3, in which case no warning fires. Non-firing = correct CB behavior. Firing = TSL correctly surfaces inertia fault.

alias_candidates.condition: if pendant is re-emitted by CB, TSL should resolve it to the existing object_id from turn 2 promotion

acquisition_signals.condition: fires only if CB re-emits pendant as player-container on the examine turn

warnings_expected.condition: fires only if CB re-emits the pendant as player-container on the examine turn with no upstream acquisition signals

world_prompt: I am in a stone temple. I am holding a silver pendant that was given to me before I entered. There is an ancient altar at the center of the room.

turns.label: place_pendant
turns.action: I set the silver pendant down on the altar.

turns.label: examine_altar
turns.action: I step back and examine the altar.
